The most significant clue is the repeated mention of "ga-ga-gayuma." This is a Filipino folk belief referring to a love potion or charm. The lyrics explicitly state the feelings are due to this "gayuma," which is deeply rooted in Filipino (and wider Southeast Asian) culture. Furthermore, references to Rebisco (a popular Filipino snack), Son Goku (a Japanese anime character), and the overall lyrical style align with contemporary Filipino music trends. The blend of Tagalog and English also points to a Southeast Asian origin, specifically the Philippines.
